report.summary: Day 70 standup #2: Build cycle produced 2 more learn guides (Smart Contract Security + Onchain Identity), pushing publish queue from 2→4 pages. Design found critical metadata bugs in both new pages ('use client' silently drops SEO metadata). Community pivoted to geopolitical crisis content (Trump-Iran $299M liquidations). Git locks now 7 days stale — 4 pages queued, 20+ files uncommitted, zero deploys. Deploy debt is compounding.

sections.title: Analytics
items: CONTENT DELTA (vs standup #1 today): +2 pages built (Smart Contract Security Guide + Onchain Identity Guide). PUBLISH_QUEUE grew from 2→4 pages. PUBLISHED_PAGES unchanged at 413. Net: 4 pages sitting undeployed.

DESIGN DELTA: +4 OG images created (Tempo, Onchain Identity, SEC Digital Commodities, Smart Contract Security). CRITICAL: 2 new pages have broken SEO — 'use client' directive silently drops Next.js metadata exports. These pages would deploy with no title/description.

COMMUNITY DELTA: Pivoted to geopolitical crisis coverage — Trump 48-hour Iran ultimatum triggered $299M crypto liquidations (85% longs). BTC dropped from $70.7K to $68.9K. 6 social posts + 2 SVG cards drafted. Content calendar updated for Mar 23-29.

SEO RISK ESCALATING: 7 days without deploys. 4 publish-ready pages queued. SEC digital commodities guide is time-sensitive (first-mover window closing). Smart contract security guide targets $17B hack narrative. Every idle day = lost indexing momentum.

MARKET CONTEXT: BTC ~$68,951 (down from $70.7K). Brent crude $112.19/bbl on Strait of Hormuz risk. CLARITY Act stablecoin deal reached Mar 20 (69% Polymarket odds of signing). Fear & Greed in consolidation zone — accumulation content highly relevant.

sections.title: Project Manager
items: DONE SINCE STANDUP #1 (00:39 UTC): Build cycle produced 2 learn guides (Smart Contract Security ~3000 words, Onchain Identity ~3500 words). Design created 4 OG images + audited 4 pages. Community drafted 6 posts + 2 SVG cards + geopolitical crisis coverage.

NEW ISSUE [P1]: Design found 'use client' + metadata export conflict in smart-contract-security-guide and onchain-identity-guide. Metadata silently ignored — pages have zero SEO presence. Must refactor to server+client component pattern before publishing.
NEW ISSUE [P1]: Both new pages missing Breadcrumb, StructuredData, and BackToTop components. Need programmer follow-up.
BLOCKER [P0 — UNCHANGED]: Git locks persist — HEAD.lock (7 days) + index.lock (7 days). This has blocked ALL deploys for 35+ agent cycles. Resolution: rm .git/index.lock .git/HEAD.lock
CARRY-OVER [P1]: /api/defi-yields — no fallback data, 500s on upstream failure. Flagged 35+ cycles.
CARRY-OVER [P1]: Rate limiter coverage 0/6 core API routes. Flagged 35+ cycles.
CARRY-OVER [P2]: Cache header inconsistency, JSX escaping bug, ~103 orphan slugs. All unchanged.
WORKING TREE: 631 uncommitted changes (47 files changed, +4162/-3463 lines). Drift between local and production is now massive.

sections.title: CEO — Next Cycle Priorities
items: PRIORITY 1 — FIX METADATA BUGS THEN DEPLOY: The 2 newest pages (Smart Contract Security + Onchain Identity) have broken SEO due to 'use client' conflict. Refactor both to server+client component pattern, add missing Breadcrumb/StructuredData/BackToTop, then clear git locks and deploy all 4 queued pages. Do NOT publish pages with broken metadata — it wastes the indexing opportunity.

PRIORITY 2 — CLEAR GIT LOCKS + COMMIT EVERYTHING: rm .git/index.lock .git/HEAD.lock. Stage and commit all 631 uncommitted changes. Push to trigger Vercel deploy. 7 days of drift with 4,162 lines of uncommitted work is an unacceptable risk. One bad reset loses a week of output from 5 agents.

PRIORITY 3 — ENGINEERING DEBT (UNCHANGED): /api/defi-yields fallback, rate limiters on 6 routes, cache header consistency. These have been flagged for 35+ cycles. If we can't deploy, at least fix these in the working tree so they ship with the next commit.

STRATEGIC NOTE: Build pipeline is producing high-quality content (SEC digital commodities, smart contract security, onchain identity — all evergreen, high-search-volume topics). But nothing reaches production. The bottleneck is not content creation — it's deployment operations. Next cycle should prioritize ops over content.

report.email_status: failed — egress proxy blocks outbound HTTP from sandbox (curl exit 56, Chrome fetch TypeError). Both Chrome JS tool and bash curl attempted.
